# Animations

::: {.callout-important}
## Experimental Feature
Animations are an **experimental feature** in MolViewSpec. The API may change in future releases. Use with caution in production environments.
:::


MolViewSpec supports animations to create dynamic, time-based visualizations that can show molecular processes, guide viewer attention, or create cinematic presentations.

::: {.callout-note}
## Current Implementation
Animations in MolViewSpec require using `ref` properties and manually modifying the snapshot. The builder API for animations is planned for a future release.
:::

## Animation Use Cases

### Scientific Storytelling
- **Molecular mechanisms**: Show conformational changes or binding events
- **Guided tours**: Direct attention to different regions sequentially
- **Process visualization**: Illustrate multi-step biochemical processes

### Presentations
- **Progressive disclosure**: Reveal structure elements one at a time
- **Emphasis**: Fade in/out or change colors to highlight features

## How Animations Work

Animations in MolViewSpec use references (`ref`) to target specific properties. You:

1. Add `ref` to properties you want to animate (color, opacity)
2. Get a snapshot from the builder
3. Manually add animation data to the snapshot
4. The animation interpolates between values over time

## Basic Animation Pattern

### Color Animation

```{.molviewspec controls=true}
const structure = builder
  .download({ url: 'https://files.wwpdb.org/download/1cbs.cif' })
  .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
  .modelStructure();

// Add color with a reference
structure
  .component({ selector: "polymer" })
  .representation({ type: "cartoon" })
  .color({ color: "blue", ref: "protein_color" });

// Get snapshot with metadata
const snapshot = builder.getSnapshot({
  title: "Color Animation",
  description: "Animating protein color from blue to cyan",
  linger_duration_ms: 5000
});

// Manually add animation
snapshot.animation = {
  kind: "animation",
  params: {
    frame_time_ms: 16.67,  // ~60fps
    autoplay: true,
    loop: false,
  },
  children: [
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "protein_color",
        property: "color",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 3000,
        kind: "color",
        palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["blue", "cyan"] },
      },
    },
  ],
};
```

### Opacity Animation

```{.molviewspec}
const structure = builder
  .download({ url: 'https://files.wwpdb.org/download/1cbs.cif' })
  .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
  .modelStructure();

// Add opacity with a reference
structure
  .component({ selector: "polymer" })
  .representation({ type: "cartoon" })
  .color({ color: "green" })
  .opacity({ opacity: 1.0, ref: "protein_opacity" });

const snapshot = builder.getSnapshot({
  title: "Opacity Animation",
  description: "Fading protein from solid to transparent",
  linger_duration_ms: 4000
});

// Fade from solid to transparent
snapshot.animation = {
  kind: "animation",
  params: {
    frame_time_ms: 16.67,
    autoplay: true,
    loop: false,
  },
  children: [
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "protein_opacity",
        property: "opacity",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 2000,
        kind: "scalar",
        start: 1.0,
        end: 0.3,
        easing: "cubic-in-out",
      },
    },
  ],
};
```

## Multiple Animations

You can animate multiple properties simultaneously:

```{.molviewspec}
const structure = builder
  .download({ url: 'https://files.wwpdb.org/download/1cbs.cif' })
  .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
  .modelStructure();

// Polymer with color and opacity references
structure
  .component({ selector: "polymer" })
  .representation({ type: "cartoon" })
  .color({ color: "blue", ref: "poly_color" })
  .opacity({ opacity: 1.0, ref: "poly_opacity" });

// Ligand with color reference
structure
  .component({ selector: "ligand" })
  .representation({ type: "ball_and_stick" })
  .color({ color: "red", ref: "lig_color" });

const snapshot = builder.getSnapshot({
  title: "Multiple Animations",
  description: "Animating polymer and ligand simultaneously",
  linger_duration_ms: 5000
});

// Animate all three properties
snapshot.animation = {
  kind: "animation",
  params: {
    frame_time_ms: 16.67,
    autoplay: true,
    loop: false,
  },
  children: [
    // Animate polymer color
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "poly_color",
        property: "color",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 3000,
        kind: "color",
        palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["blue", "cyan"] },
      },
    },
    // Animate polymer opacity
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "poly_opacity",
        property: "opacity",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 3000,
        kind: "scalar",
        start: 1.0,
        end: 0.5,
        easing: "cubic-in-out",
      },
    },
    // Animate ligand color (delayed start)
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "lig_color",
        property: "color",
        start_ms: 1000,  // Start after 1 second
        duration_ms: 2000,
        kind: "color",
        palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["red", "yellow"] },
      },
    },
  ],
};
```

## Animation Parameters

### Animation Root

- `frame_time_ms`: Time per frame in milliseconds (16.67 ≈ 60fps)
- `autoplay`: Start animation automatically
- `loop`: Whether to loop the animation

### Interpolate Node

**Common Parameters**:
- `target_ref`: Reference to the property to animate
- `property`: Property name (`"color"` or `"opacity"`)
- `start_ms`: When to start (milliseconds)
- `duration_ms`: Animation duration (milliseconds)

**For Color Animations** (`kind: "color"`):
- `palette`: Color gradient
  - `kind: "continuous"`: Smooth gradient
  - `colors`: Array of color values

**For Scalar Animations** (`kind: "scalar"`):
- `start`: Starting value
- `end`: Ending value
- `easing`: Easing function (optional)

## Easing Functions

Control the acceleration of animations:

- **linear**: Constant speed
- **cubic-in**: Start slow, accelerate
- **cubic-out**: Start fast, decelerate
- **cubic-in-out**: Start and end slow, fast in middle
- **quadratic-in**, **quadratic-out**, **quadratic-in-out**
- **exponential-in**, **exponential-out**, **exponential-in-out**

```javascript
{
  kind: "interpolate",
  params: {
    // ... other params
    easing: "cubic-in-out",
  }
}
```

## Sequential Animations

Use `start_ms` to create sequences:

```{.molviewspec controls=true}
const structure = builder
  .download({ url: 'https://files.wwpdb.org/download/1cbs.cif' })
  .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
  .modelStructure();

structure
  .component({ selector: "polymer" })
  .representation({ type: "cartoon" })
  .color({ color: "blue", ref: "color_1" });

const snapshot = builder.getSnapshot({
  title: "Sequential Color Changes",
  description: "Blue to green to red sequence",
  linger_duration_ms: 5000
});

snapshot.animation = {
  kind: "animation",
  params: { frame_time_ms: 16.67, autoplay: true, loop: false },
  children: [
    // First: Blue to green (0-2s)
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "color_1",
        property: "color",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 2000,
        kind: "color",
        palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["blue", "green"] },
      },
    },
    // Then: Green to red (2-4s)
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "color_1",
        property: "color",
        start_ms: 2000,
        duration_ms: 2000,
        kind: "color",
        palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["green", "red"] },
      },
    },
  ],
};
```

## Progressive Reveal Pattern

Fade in components one by one:

```{.molviewspec}
const structure = builder
  .download({ url: 'https://files.wwpdb.org/download/1cbs.cif' })
  .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
  .modelStructure();

// Start all invisible
structure
  .component({ selector: "polymer" })
  .representation({ type: "cartoon" })
  .color({ color: "blue" })
  .opacity({ opacity: 0.0, ref: "poly_opacity" });

structure
  .component({ selector: "ligand" })
  .representation({ type: "ball_and_stick" })
  .color({ color: "red" })
  .opacity({ opacity: 0.0, ref: "lig_opacity" });

const snapshot = builder.getSnapshot({
  title: "Progressive Reveal",
  description: "Fade in polymer then ligand",
  linger_duration_ms: 5000
});

snapshot.animation = {
  kind: "animation",
  params: { frame_time_ms: 16.67, autoplay: true, loop: false },
  children: [
    // Reveal polymer first (0-2s)
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "poly_opacity",
        property: "opacity",
        start_ms: 0,
        duration_ms: 2000,
        kind: "scalar",
        start: 0.0,
        end: 1.0,
        easing: "cubic-out",
      },
    },
    // Then reveal ligand (2.5-3.5s)
    {
      kind: "interpolate",
      params: {
        target_ref: "lig_opacity",
        property: "opacity",
        start_ms: 2500,
        duration_ms: 1000,
        kind: "scalar",
        start: 0.0,
        end: 1.0,
        easing: "cubic-out",
      },
    },
  ],
};
```

## Animation in MolViewStories

::: {.callout-note}
## Story Scenes as Animation Frames
In MolViewStories, each scene can represent a "frame" in your narrative. Scene transitions provide a higher-level alternative to MolViewSpec animations.
:::

MolViewStories provides complementary approaches:
- **Scene transitions**: Move between different states
- **Scene-specific cameras**: Different viewpoints per scene
- **Progressive scenes**: Build complexity across multiple scenes
- **MolViewSpec animations**: Smooth transitions within a single scene

## Performance Tips

::: {.callout-warning}
## Animation Performance
- **Short durations**: Keep animations under 3-5 seconds for best UX
- **Limit simultaneous animations**: Too many at once can cause lag
- **Frame rate**: 16.67ms (60fps) is smooth; 33ms (30fps) is acceptable
- **Simple representations**: Cartoon animates faster than surface
:::

## Accessibility Considerations

::: {.callout-important}
## Motion Sensitivity
Some users may be sensitive to motion. Consider:
- Using the `autoplay: false` option
- Providing slower transitions by default
- Avoiding rapid color changes or flashing
- Including a note that animations are present
:::

## Future API

::: {.callout-note}
## Builder API Coming Soon
A builder-based API for animations is planned:

```javascript
// Future API (not yet available)
const anim = builder.animation();

anim.interpolate({
  kind: "color",
  target_ref: "protein_color",
  property: "color",
  duration_ms: 3000,
  palette: { kind: "continuous", colors: ["blue", "cyan"] },
});
```

Currently, use the manual snapshot modification approach shown above.
:::

## See Also

- [Camera Settings](camera.qmd) - Camera positioning
- [Tree Schema](mvs-spec.qmd) - Complete node reference
- [Examples](examples.qmd) - More interactive examples
